title: Execute a verified extended-precision run (target sized to justified donated budget); full HARD-RULE verification
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The HARD RULE binds all computation-run and release work: no digit is accepted or published unless two independent computations are bit-identical, BBP/Bellard hex spot-checks pass, every chunk is recompute-verified and hashed, the published prefix matches a known reference, and the guard-digit analysis is recorded; an author may not verify their own run. This run's digit target and total compute/energy budget must be approved in advance against the energy-cost disclosure (no open-ended chase), it must pass the full HARD RULE signed by the verify-steward (not the runner), and zero unverified digits may be published. It depends on coord-011 and expert-016.
objective: Execute a verified extended-precision pi run, sized to a justified donated budget, that passes the full HARD RULE with verify-steward sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: The run's digit target and total compute/energy budget were approved in advance against the energy-cost disclosure (no open-ended chase).

The result passes the full HARD RULE (two-method bit-identical agreement + BBP spot-checks + every chunk recompute-verified + reference prefix match + guard-digit analysis), signed by the verify-steward (not the runner).
output: A dataset of the verified extended-precision pi run with its VerificationRecord and budget approval.
